title: Cymbidium aberrans
taxonomy: Cymbidium aberrans is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Cymbidium. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which includes approximately 30,000 species of orchids. Cymbidium aberrans is further classified as part of the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cymbidieae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its flowers, leaves, and growth habits. As a member of the Cymbidium genus, Cymbidium aberrans is native to the subtropical regions of southern China and Southeast Asia.

intro: Cymbidium aberrans, also known as the aberrant cymbidium, is a species of orchid native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Cymbidium aberrans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as contributing to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cymbidium aberrans is often prepared as a tea or decoction, as well as being used in the production of dried extracts and capsules, to support respiratory health and immune function.
